exemplar.title: To what extent has Liberty Mediaâs acquisition of Formula One impacted its financial growth in the United States?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Liberty Mediaâs acquisition of Formula One impacted its financial growth in the United States?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is explicitly stated, well focused, and directly drives subsequent analysis
Title and introduction clearly communicate the topic and its relevance for a non-specialist reader
Methodology identifies appropriate analytical tools (ratio analysis, PESTLE, Ansoff Matrix) with rationale for their selection
weaknesses: No primary research or triangulation strategy is described
Explanation of how each analytical tool links to the research question is brief
Range of sources is adequate but lacks diversity beyond secondary data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication and question formulation at the 5â6 band, while research methodology sits in the 3â4 band. Overall, the investigation communicates its focus accurately and selects relevant methods, but omission of primary data and brevity in methodological justification prevent full marks. A score of 5/6 reflects strong focus and method with minor gaps.

strengths: Tables and revenue figures are generally pertinent and well integrated into the argument
Clear and coherent knowledge of PESTLE, Ansoff Matrix, and ratio analysis frameworks
Terminology (e.g. market penetration, adjusted OIBDA) is used accurately and in context
weaknesses: Occasional unsupported or speculative claims (e.g. sustainabilityâsponsorship link)
Numerical formatting errors in adjusted OIBDA figures undermine clarity
Some descriptive passages dominate, reducing analytical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (application of sources, subject knowledge, terminology) consistently align with the 3â4 band: evidence is mostly relevant, knowledge coherent, and terminology correct. Occasional inaccuracies and descriptive dominance limit higher achievement, justifying a 4/6.

strengths: Wide variety of secondary sources consistently relevant to the research question
Effective contextual comparisons with NFL and NBA revenues
Calculations of percentage changes clearly support key arguments
weaknesses: Source credibility is not critically evaluated and no primary data are incorporated
Analysis in PESTLE and Ansoff sections remains largely descriptive
Evaluation of risks (e.g. economic downturn) lacks depth and quantification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality aligns with the 7â9 band, while analysis depth and evaluation fall in the 4â6 band. The work exhibits strong research relevance and clear analytical moves, but lacks sophisticated analysis and critical evaluation. A score of 7/12 reflects this mixture of strengths and limitations.

strengths: Required structural elements (word count, title, pagination, contents page) are all present
Overall document structure follows IB expectations and supports navigability
weaknesses: Formatting inconsistencies (misaligned tables, broken sentences, image placeholders without captions)
Inconsistent indentation and truncated headings reduce visual coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure appropriateness is in the 3â4 band, while layout and formatting are closer to the 1â2 band. Despite evident structural organization, multiple formatting lapses prevent the highest score. A 4/4 acknowledges strong structural compliance but penalizes presentation flaws.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question â âTo what extent has Liberty Mediaâs acquisition of Formula One impacted its financial growth in the United States?â â is explicitly stated, narrow in scope (time-bounded to 2017-23 and geographically to the U S.) and directly drives the subsequent sections (PESTLE, Ansoff, ratio tables and financial data all relate to U S. growth).  Because the discussion never drifts into unrelated areas (e.g., team performance or global fan culture), the focus is maintained throughout.  A slight overlap with global considerations prevents a 6, but the question is nevertheless clear, well-focused, and effectively linked to analysis.

Arguments are logically organised and a concluding judgment is presented, yet evaluation is intermittent; for example, risks such as economic downturn are mentioned but not explored in depth, and benefits are seldom weighed against costs quantitatively.  The discussion therefore displays a well-developed argument with only partially critical evaluation.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of business-management frameworks (PESTLE, Ansoff, ratio analysis) and of sports-media revenue models, applying these concepts coherently.  Nevertheless, some descriptive passages dominate (large blocks of narrative about U S. race culture) and a few numerical errors appear (OIBDA figure expressed with too many zeros).  Knowledge is therefore clear but not consistently integrated at an advanced level.

Terminology such as âmarket penetration,â âbroadcast rights,â âadjusted OIBDA,â and âexternal environmentâ is used accurately and in context.  Sporadic typographical issues (âgrewâ for âgrownâ, sentence fragments in the Ansoff table) interrupt consistency, so the strand fits the âmostly accurateâ descriptor rather than the top band.

The student calculates percentage revenue changes, links Ansoff strategies to concrete actions, and contrasts F1 figures with NFL/NBA benchmarks.  Counter-arguments about ticket pricing and race-time inconvenience show some balance.  Still, several analytical sections remain descriptive (long narrative lists in the PESTLE and Ansoff tables) and some figures are not fully interpreted, so the analysis is clear and partly supported but not yet âsophisticated.â

Tables, revenue figures, viewership statistics and citations are used throughout the argument and are generally pertinent to financial growth in the U S.; for instance, Table 1 and the ESPN rights fee discussion directly support the thesis.  Occasional unsupported claims (e.g., speculative links between sustainability and sponsorship) lower consistency, but overall the application of evidence is mostly relevant and appropriate.

The methodology section justifies the choice of secondary data, ratio analysis, PESTLE and Ansoff Matrix and names annual reports and academic databases as sources, showing informed selection.  However, no primary research or triangulation strategy is offered, and the explanation of how each tool will connect to the question is brief.  The range of sources is adequate but not wide (almost all are online articles and company filings).  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band rather than the top band.

A wide variety of secondary sources is consulted â company filings, Statista data, academic journals, press releases â and almost all relate directly to U S. financial performance post-acquisition.  Source triangulation is uneven and there is little primary data, preventing excellence, but the breadth and relevance exceed the adequacy threshold and meet the lower end of the 7-9 band.

A word count, title and pagination are present, and MLA-style citations appear; however, formatting inconsistencies aboundâimage placeholders, broken sentences, and mis-aligned tablesâindicating only partial adherence to IB layout conventions.  These lapses place the work in the 1-2 band with the higher score because the basic elements are still observable.

The student states the title, session, and research question on the cover page and reiterates the focus clearly in the introduction.  The background paragraph explains Liberty Mediaâs 2017 takeover and outlines why U S. financial performance is worth investigating, providing sufficient contextual detail for a non-specialist reader.  Minor stylistic lapses and some missing transition sentences reduce the overall polish, so the work does not reach the very top of the band, but topic communication is still accurate, purposeful, and complete.

The essay follows the required EE structure (title page, abstract omitted but not mandatory, contents page, numbered sections, works cited) and main sections flow logically.  Nevertheless, the Ansoff table is split over pages and some headings are repeated or truncated, showing minor structural weaknesses; hence a mid-band award within 3-4 is justified.

annotations.comment: Contents page lists all required sections in a logical order, supporting navigability.
Indentation for sub-sections in the TOC is inconsistent, affecting visual coherence.
Research question is explicitly stated and focused on US financial growth post-acquisition.
Minor grammatical error: âhas successfully grewâ should be âhas successfully grown.â
Social factors heading runs into technology text, impairing readability; separate clearly.
Title is clear and specific, effectively communicating the research focus.
Word count is provided and within the 4000-word limit, meeting IB formatting requirements.
Image placeholder lacks a clear caption or source, reducing clarity and professional polish.
Methodology clearly outlines ratio analysis, Ansoff Matrix, and PESTLE as relevant analytical tools.
No primary data or triangulation strategy is described; consider adding interviews or surveys.
Economic PESTLE section uses concrete ticket-price and sales figures to support claims.
Source credibility is not evaluated; discuss the reliability of Aginiâs data.
Risk discussion notes economic downturn but lacks quantification of its financial impact.
Social factors section links Drive to Surviveâs launch to a 40% US viewership increase.
Entries in Market Development and Diversification are descriptive; strengthen by linking to measured outcomes.
Legal factors include specific viewership figures, strengthening the argument quantitatively.
Ansoff Matrix spans multiple pages and is overcrowded; consider splitting and summarizing key points.
The 2488.89% change in 2021 appears implausible; verify calculation or add explanatory footnote.
Comparisons with NFL and NBA revenues effectively contextualize F1âs growth within US sports.
Conclusion restates findings but offers limited critical reflection on research limitations.
Consider adding reflective commentary on methodological challenges and future data needs.
Table 1 presents clear year-on-year revenue changes with percentage calculations.
Revenue definition text does not address how 2020 outlier is incorporated; discuss impact on trend analysis.
Adjusted OIBDA values are mis-formatted (e.g., â397,000,00â missing a digit), undermining quantitative clarity.
Works Cited is extensive but formatting varies (e.g., inconsistent italics and punctuation).
While secondary sources are varied, primary research is absent; consider interviews or survey data.
